extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Proof by inductive invariant: opted_out becomes true ONLY via t_optout (on optout, guard true), which atomically also sets sent := false — so immediately after opted_out turns true, sent is false. While opted_out remains true, no transition can set sent to true: the only transition setting sent := true is t_send_allowed, whose guard is "not opted_out", so it is disabled whenever opted_out is true. Every other event that can fire while opted_out is true (send_attempt -> t_send_blocked, tick -> t_tick, repeated optout -> t_optout) sets sent := false, never true. The initial state has opted_out=false, satisfying the implication vacuously. Therefore in every reachable state opted_out=true implies sent=false, and no counterexample exists within 8 events (or any length).
